behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real filesystem watcher recursion behavior and must prove an externally visible side effect (file is actually processed and emitted to output) via round-trip readback. Unit/integration fakes can miss OS-specific event delivery for nested paths. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by the behavior text, which contains no AI/ML step.
behaviors.test_description: Start the real daemon against real input/output directories. Create `input/subdir/test.txt`, then assert exactly one corresponding transformed output appears and is readable from the output directory within timeout.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: The likely risk is module handoff/config wiring (request ingestion + dedup store + time-window config), not necessarily real OS/network/subprocess behavior. System was considered but rejected because the behavior text only specifies dedup of repeated requests for the same path/window, not OS-specific event semantics or external contract/error-shape checks. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by no AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real internal modules in-process (ingest path -> dedup component -> scheduler/pipeline stub). Submit the same path twice within the configured window and assert only one downstream processing invocation occurs; submit again after the window and assert processing occurs.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: The behavior explicitly names OS multi-event emission; this is real infrastructure and can be hidden by mocked watchers. It also requires proving external side effect count (exactly one processed output) via round-trip observation. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by no AI/ML behavior in the text.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real daemon with a real watched directory. Perform a write pattern known to trigger multiple events (e.g., temp-write + rename or chunked write). Verify only one transformed output artifact is produced/readable for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: Directory deletion/recreation while live is OS-level watcher behavior and requires real filesystem events. Success must be shown by external round-trip side effect after recreation (new file is processed without restart). Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no model decisions are involved.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon watching `input/`. Delete `input/`, recreate `input/`, then place a new file in recreated directory. Assert daemon processes it successfully without process restart and output is present/readable.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering here depends on real filesystem timestamp semantics/event timing plus daemon scheduling, and correctness is externally visible in output processing order. Mocked time/events can hide failures tied to OS timestamp granularity or event interleaving. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by lack of AI/ML dependency in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run real daemon on real filesystem. Create multiple files nearly simultaneously with controlled creation times, then verify observed processing/output order matches creation timestamp order from filesystem metadata and emitted results.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: This is a classic real filesystem timing/partial-write race; mocked I/O often cannot reproduce the failure. The behavior requires external round-trip validation that processing starts only after completion and output corresponds to full file contents. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by no AI/ML model in scope.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon with real watcher. Begin slow/chunked write to a large file while keeping handle open; trigger arrival event naturally. Assert no processing/output appears during write, then close file and assert processing occurs once with full expected transformed content.
